content.text:
I'm working in the GAIN Agentic Investigation Challenge repo at
C:\Users\rcalv\Projects\Northwestern Project\gain-investigation (deadline 2026-07-15).

Read CLAUDE.md, LEDGER.md, and DECISIONS.md first. Work on a new git branch
(e.g. `feature/press-issue-frequency`) — other sessions may be working in parallel on
other gaps against the same DB and ledger, so don't commit to main until told to merge.

Gap from data_manual.md this covers: press "keyword and frequency analysis" (who talks
about a given issue, how often, when does language shift) and the cross-dataset
"temporal lobbying-messaging coupling" question (does press volume on an issue track
lobbying spend on that issue).

Task:
1. Extend the extraction pattern already proven in build_db.py's `extract_bills()` (see
   `bill_mentions` table) to also tag press releases with ALI issue codes — use
   `data/senate/constants/lobbying_activity_issues.json` as your keyword vocabulary
   (start from the code's own name/description, e.g. HCR="Health Issues", and build a
   reasonable keyword-to-code mapping; document exactly what maps to what, since this
   determines every downstream count). Write results to a new table, e.g.
   `press_issue_mentions(pr_id, issue_code, keyword, src_file, src_line)` — same
   raw-record-pointer discipline as every other table (see CLAUDE.md's loader
   guarantees).
2. Once that table exists, join it against the existing `v_spend_by_issue_quarter` view
   (already defined in build_db.py's VIEWS) to test: for a given issue code, does press
   mention volume spike before, after, or alongside lobbying spend spikes on that code?
   Do this per-quarter, across the full corpus (db/lda_full.duckdb).
3. Anything that looks like a real, citable coupling (or a real, citable ABSENCE of
   coupling where you'd expect one) becomes a candidate lead in LEDGER.md — named actor,
   date, record ID, same rules as everything else in this repo.
4. Note in your write-up which keyword-to-code mappings are shaky/ambiguous — that's a
   caveat future readers need, not a thing to hide.

Smoke test before and after. Lint LEDGER.md after any edits. Export the trace, commit to
your branch, tell me when it's ready to review/merge.

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - API usage, tool use, Anthropic SDK usage. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Glob, Grep, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Read-only search agent for broad fan-out searches — when answering means sweeping many files, directories, or naming conventions and you only need the conclusion, not the file dumps. It reads excerpts rather than whole files, so it locates code; it doesn't review or audit it. Specify search breadth: "medium" for moderate exploration, "very thorough" for multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by exercising it end-to-end and observing behavior — drive the affected flow, not just tests or typecheck. Run before committing nontrivial changes; bootstraps this repo's project verify skill if none exists yet. Don't invoke it on a diff that only touches tests, docs, or other code with no runtime surface to drive (a change to product source always has one) — there's nothing to observe.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: I'll start by reading the key files to understand the repo conventions, then explore the structure.

content.content:
1	# GAIN Agentic Investigation Challenge — workspace guide
2	
3	Submission workspace for Northwestern's GAIN Agentic Investigation Challenge: an agentic
4	investigation of congressional lobbying filings (Senate + House LDA) and congressional press
5	releases, 2022–2026 Q1, asking who is paying whom to shape policy. **Deadline: 2026-07-15.**
6	README.md is the submission map (skills → findings → traces); this file is the working guide.
7	
8	## Layout
9	
10	```
11	gain-investigation/
12	├── README.md            ← submission map (six required elements; fill in as work happens)
13	├── CLAUDE.md            ← this file
14	├── LEDGER.md            ← investigation state: leads / entities checked / queries run / cold threads
15	├── DECISIONS.md         ← human-judgment log (required by the traces deliverable)
16	├── archive/             ← reset snapshots of LEDGER.md/DECISIONS.md (see note below); not submitted
17	├── skills/              ← the submitted Agent Skills, each self-contained (SKILL.md + scripts/)
18	│   ├── lda-corpus-loader/     build_db.py (raw corpus → DuckDB) + show_record.py (citation → raw record)
19	│   ├── lda-entity-resolver/   cross-dataset entity table (Senate↔House crosswalk)
20	│   ├── investigation-ledger/  ledger templates + ledger_lint.py
21	│   ├── lead-scanner/          lens SQL library + scanning discipline
22	│   ├── finding-verifier/      pre-lock claim re-derivation protocol
23	│   └── outside-context-scan/  exploratory novelty/news-landscape research (not verification)
24	├── queries/             ← lens SQL (sweep_2026.sql + run_sweep.py); cited by aggregate claims
25	├── findings/            ← one locked finding per file (locked = verification passed)
26	├── traces/              ← session JSONL exports + INDEX.md + rendered/ HTML
27	├── tests/               ← smoke_test.py + fixtures/ (tiny excerpts of real public records)
28	├── data/                ← raw corpus (gitignored; layout per the challenge data manual)
29	└── db/                  ← built DuckDB files (gitignored; rebuilt from raw via loader)
30	```
31	
32	## Commands (Windows dev box: `.venv/Scripts/python`; POSIX: `.venv/bin/python`)
33	
34	```bash
35	python -m venv .venv && .venv/Scripts/python -m pip install -r requirements.txt
36	.venv/Scripts/python tests/smoke_test.py                                  # green before anything else
37	.venv/Scripts/python skills/lda-corpus-loader/scripts/build_db.py \
38	    --data-root data/ --db db/lda.duckdb [--years 2025 2026] [--sample 2025-Q1]
39	.venv/Scripts/python skills/lda-corpus-loader/scripts/show_record.py <citation-key>
40	.venv/Scripts/python queries/run_sweep.py db/lda_full.duckdb [BLOCK-PREFIX]
41	.venv/Scripts/python skills/investigation-ledger/scripts/ledger_lint.py LEDGER.md
42	```
43	
44	Citation keys: Senate `filing_uuid` · House numeric XML filename (e.g. `301817772`) ·
45	press `src_file:src_line` (e.g. `congress_press/2026-01.jsonl:12`) — all stable identifiers
46	from the source systems, valid regardless of which DB below they were queried through.
47	
48	**Corpus versions** (all built the same way, differing only by `--years`; see
49	`skills/lda-corpus-loader/scripts/build_db.py`):
50	- `db/lda_full.duckdb` — 2022–2026, all years. **Canonical/primary as of 2026-07-06.**
51	  Start new investigative work here.
52	- `db/lda_pilot.duckdb` — 2025 + 2026-Q1 only. Kept solely to reproduce
53	  `findings/L010_pipe_materials_war.md`'s citations exactly as verified; not for new work.
54	- `db/lda_2026.duckdb` — 2026-Q1 only. Superseded by both of the above; safe to delete
55	  locally, nothing cites it that the other two can't also resolve.
56	
57	## Load-bearing conventions
58	
59	- **Never cite the database.** Record-level claims cite citation keys resolvable via
60	  `show_record.py`. Aggregate claims (spikes, rankings, counts) cite three things: the exact SQL
61	  (a labeled block in `queries/`), the one-command DB rebuild, and ≥3 sampled underlying records.
62	- **Raw corpus access goes through `show_record.py` only.** Never grep or open raw corpus files
63	  directly — 409K XMLs will eat your context and your citations won't be reproducible.
64	- **Every DB row carries a raw-record pointer** (`src_file` + `src_line`/`src_index`, or XML
65	  path). If you add a table, preserve this invariant.
66	- **Ledger discipline (single-writer).** Read `LEDGER.md` at session start; commit updates at
67	  session end and on any status change. Sub-agents return candidate rows; only the orchestrating
68	  session writes. Statuses: `open → triaged → investigating → verified | dead`, plus `parked`
69	  (cold, with revisit trigger). Run `ledger_lint.py` after edits.
70	- **Named-actor rule.** A lead with no specific actor, date, and record ID cannot pass triage.
71	  "Spend on issue X spiked" is a dataset summary; name who, when, and which filings.
72	- **DECISIONS.md** gets a row for every human intervention (triage picks, kills, editorial and
73	  legal-flag calls) with the trace file it happened in.
74	- **Traces.** Every working session is exported to `traces/YYYY-MM-DD_<skill-or-phase>_<lead>.jsonl`
75	  (session JSONL lives under `~/.claude/projects/<project-slug>/`) and indexed in `traces/INDEX.md`.
76	- **Findings lock only after verification.** A fresh session (no drafting context) re-derives
77	  every claim from cited records per `skills/finding-verifier/SKILL.md`, then the lock is logged
78	  in `DECISIONS.md`. The findings report is assembled from locked findings only.
79	- **Model/budget tiers.** Extraction and filtering stay in Python/SQL. Cheap models scan and rank
80	  (returning record IDs + one-line hypotheses, never quoted record text). Frontier models
81	  deep-read lead-attached records (cap ~30 records/session) and verify findings.
82	- **Exploratory outside-data checks (novelty/news-landscape) follow a fixed protocol,
83	  separate from finding-verifier's rigor** — anchor the search window to the actual event
84	  date (not "today"), run novelty and landscape checks as separate bounded passes (~3
85	  queries each), distill to a conclusion before it reaches the ledger. Full protocol in
86	  `skills/outside-context-scan/SKILL.md`. Established 2026-07-06 after searching
87	  "GlobalFoundries news 2026" for a 2024 event; deliberately kept out of
88	  `skills/finding-verifier/SKILL.md` since "verifier" implies a rigor this exploratory
89	  research doesn't have and shouldn't claim.
90	- **Self-contained language.** Submitted artifacts (SKILL.md files, findings, README) must stand
91	  alone — no references to internal planning docs or prior processes.
92	
93	## Data facts that bite (verified against real records 2026-07-04)
94	
95	- House quarterly XML uses `<alis><ali_info>` with `<issueAreaCode>` — NOT the `ali_Code` flat
96	  list the data manual describes (that may still apply to LD-1 registrations; unverified).
97	- Agencies lobbied are one comma-separated `<federal_agencies>` string per ali_info; splitting
98	  it is entity-resolver work.
99	- House forms pad with empty `<lobbyist>` slots — skip rows with no first and no last name.
100	- Senate registrant objects carry `house_registrant_id` — the clean Senate↔House crosswalk key.
101	- Senate LD-203 contributions are semiannual; early-year files are legitimately tiny.
102	- **Never sum filings without deduping — and the dedup key must be the period-invariant
103	  field, not the type code, applied identically on both chambers.** Registrants file
104	  duplicates (identical Senate Q1s posted 22 seconds apart) and amendments (Senate
105	  `filing_type` codes like `1A`/`2A`/`3A`/`4A`; House refilings under new filing_ids).
106	  Filtering senate to `filing_type LIKE 'Q%'` SILENTLY DROPS amendments — this bit twice:
107	  2026-07-04's un-deduped version fabricated a "House reports 2× Senate" pattern, and
108	  2026-07-06's H1c/H1d v1 (which filtered/partitioned by `filing_type`) fabricated a
109	  "chronic 10x cross-chamber mis-reporter" pattern that was actually senate's
110	  PRE-amendment figure vs house's POST-amendment figure (house wasn't affected because its
111	  directory-based period bucketing already folds originals+amendments together). Caught
112	  by a human cross-checking the live House portal, not by any internal check — a reminder
113	  that "the query ran and produced clean-looking numbers" is not verification. The correct
114	  pattern: dedup keyed on `filing_period` (constant across original+amendment, e.g.
115	  "second_quarter" for both `Q2` and `2A`), NOT `filing_type`, picking latest by `posted`/
116	  `filing_id` — see `queries/sweep_2026.sql#H1c` (current, fixed version) for the pattern.
117	- **Never sum senate + house datasets.** LD-2 quarterlies are filed with both chambers, so the
118	  two datasets are largely copies of the same filings. Dollar attribution is senate-primary
119	  (richer metadata); use house only to reconcile or fill gaps. Verified 2026-07-05 — the
120	  cross-dataset version (sweep#C1b) inflated per-bill totals ~40%.
121	- **House XML dumps are partial snapshots.** House 2026-Q1 holds 12,656 filings vs 21,145
122	  senate Q1s (the deadline-week flood is missing). Senate is the completeness reference for
123	  recent quarters; a filing absent house-side is expected noise, not a story.
124	- **Press and filings name bills differently.** Members write "the Farm Bill" / "NDAA"; filings
125	  cite H.R./S. numbers. Number-only matching fabricates "lobbied but publicly silent" bills
126	  (killed L004 that way). Say-vs-pay comparisons need alias matching or provision-level framing.
127	- **The Senate↔House join key is house `<senateID>` = `"<senate_registrant_id>-<senate_client_id>"`**
128	  (compound, engagement-level; verified 2026-07-06). Do NOT join senate `house_registrant_id`
129	  to house `<houseID>` — formats don't overlap (zero matches). Use `registrant_crosswalk`
130	  (entity-resolver) or the split_part pattern in `queries/sweep_2026.sql#H1c`.
131	- **`<houseID>` is NOT a resolvable filing_id/filename, and can collide with an unrelated
132	  one.** Verified 2026-07-06: a Mercury/PRVO Plinarsko Društvo quarterly filing carries
133	  `<houseID>301740622</houseID>`, but filing `301740622.xml` in this corpus is a completely
134	  different, unrelated registration (Cornerstone Government Affairs / National Association
135	  of Home Builders). `<houseID>` appears to be a persistent House-Clerk identifier for the
136	  registrant-client relationship (what the House disclosure portal's own search surfaces) —
137	  a different ID namespace from `filing_id` (the numeric filename), even though both are
138	  9-digit numbers that can coincidentally match an unrelated document. Never cite a
139	  `<houseID>` value as a `show_record.py` key; only the filename (`filing_id`) is a valid
140	  citation key on the House side.
141	- **Senate `client_id` is registrant-scoped, not global** — Comcast alone has 10+ client ids.
142	  Group clients by resolved entity (`entities`/`entity_aliases`, norm-key based), never by
143	  client_id. Registrant ids ARE global.
144	- Everything self-reported: strip whitespace everywhere, expect missing income/expenses, and
145	  treat gaps as potentially reportable rather than as noise.
146	- Windows: scripts force UTF-8 stdout (press text has curly quotes; pipes default to cp1252).
147	
148	## Session discipline for Claude sessions in this repo
149	
150	1. Start: read this file, `LEDGER.md`, and the tail of `DECISIONS.md`. Don't re-derive state.
151	2. Work one lead or one phase per session where practical (keeps traces reviewable).
152	3. End: update `LEDGER.md` (+ lint), log any human decisions, export the session trace, update
153	   `traces/INDEX.md`, commit.
154	
155	Internal planning context (not part of the submission, not for citation in submitted artifacts):
156	`../_Plan.md` in the parent folder holds the phased plan and decision register.
157	
158	**2026-07-06 reset:** `LEDGER.md`/`DECISIONS.md` were emptied and the prior pilot-scale content
159	moved to `archive/*_pilot-triage_2026-07-06.md`, so a session working the newly-built full corpus
160	(`db/lda_full.duckdb`) starts with a clean, unbiased view rather than inheriting pilot-era lead
161	framing. This was a deliberate one-time QA reset, not a standing practice — don't repeat it on a
162	future corpus rebuild without being asked. The archived files are historical reference only; do
163	not read them into context unless specifically asked to reconcile or restore something from them
164	(e.g. `findings/L010_pipe_materials_war.md` is untouched and still a real, independently-verified
165	finding regardless of this reset — it just isn't re-listed as a lead in the fresh ledger).
166

# GAIN Agentic Investigation Challenge — workspace guide

Submission workspace for Northwestern's GAIN Agentic Investigation Challenge: an agentic
investigation of congressional lobbying filings (Senate + House LDA) and congressional press
releases, 2022–2026 Q1, asking who is paying whom to shape policy. **Deadline: 2026-07-15.**
README.md is the submission map (skills → findings → traces); this file is the working guide.

## Layout

```
gain-investigation/
├── README.md            ← submission map (six required elements; fill in as work happens)
├── CLAUDE.md            ← this file
├── LEDGER.md            ← investigation state: leads / entities checked / queries run / cold threads
├── DECISIONS.md         ← human-judgment log (required by the traces deliverable)
├── archive/             ← reset snapshots of LEDGER.md/DECISIONS.md (see note below); not submitted
├── skills/              ← the submitted Agent Skills, each self-contained (SKILL.md + scripts/)
│   ├── lda-corpus-loader/     build_db.py (raw corpus → DuckDB) + show_record.py (citation → raw record)
│   ├── lda-entity-resolver/   cross-dataset entity table (Senate↔House crosswalk)
│   ├── investigation-ledger/  ledger templates + ledger_lint.py
│   ├── lead-scanner/          lens SQL library + scanning discipline
│   ├── finding-verifier/      pre-lock claim re-derivation protocol
│   └── outside-context-scan/  exploratory novelty/news-landscape research (not verification)
├── queries/             ← lens SQL (sweep_2026.sql + run_sweep.py); cited by aggregate claims
├── findings/            ← one locked finding per file (locked = verification passed)
├── traces/              ← session JSONL exports + INDEX.md + rendered/ HTML
├── tests/               ← smoke_test.py + fixtures/ (tiny excerpts of real public records)
├── data/                ← raw corpus (gitignored; layout per the challenge data manual)
└── db/                  ← built DuckDB files (gitignored; rebuilt from raw via loader)
```

## Commands (Windows dev box: `.venv/Scripts/python`; POSIX: `.venv/bin/python`)

```bash
python -m venv .venv && .venv/Scripts/python -m pip install -r requirements.txt
.venv/Scripts/python tests/smoke_test.py                                  # green before anything else
.venv/Scripts/python skills/lda-corpus-loader/scripts/build_db.py \
    --data-root data/ --db db/lda.duckdb [--years 2025 2026] [--sample 2025-Q1]
.venv/Scripts/python skills/lda-corpus-loader/scripts/show_record.py <citation-key>
.venv/Scripts/python queries/run_sweep.py db/lda_full.duckdb [BLOCK-PREFIX]
.venv/Scripts/python skills/investigation-ledger/scripts/ledger_lint.py LEDGER.md
```

Citation keys: Senate `filing_uuid` · House numeric XML filename (e.g. `301817772`) ·
press `src_file:src_line` (e.g. `congress_press/2026-01.jsonl:12`) — all stable identifiers
from the source systems, valid regardless of which DB below they were queried through.

**Corpus versions** (all built the same way, differing only by `--years`; see
`skills/lda-corpus-loader/scripts/build_db.py`):
- `db/lda_full.duckdb` — 2022–2026, all years. **Canonical/primary as of 2026-07-06.**
  Start new investigative work here.
- `db/lda_pilot.duckdb` — 2025 + 2026-Q1 only. Kept solely to reproduce
  `findings/L010_pipe_materials_war.md`'s citations exactly as verified; not for new work.
- `db/lda_2026.duckdb` — 2026-Q1 only. Superseded by both of the above; safe to delete
  locally, nothing cites it that the other two can't also resolve.

## Load-bearing conventions

- **Never cite the database.** Record-level claims cite citation keys resolvable via
  `show_record.py`. Aggregate claims (spikes, rankings, counts) cite three things: the exact SQL
  (a labeled block in `queries/`), the one-command DB rebuild, and ≥3 sampled underlying records.
- **Raw corpus access goes through `show_record.py` only.** Never grep or open raw corpus files
  directly — 409K XMLs will eat your context and your citations won't be reproducible.
- **Every DB row carries a raw-record pointer** (`src_file` + `src_line`/`src_index`, or XML
  path). If you add a table, preserve this invariant.
- **Ledger discipline (single-writer).** Read `LEDGER.md` at session start; commit updates at
  session end and on any status change. Sub-agents return candidate rows; only the orchestrating
  session writes. Statuses: `open → triaged → investigating → verified | dead`, plus `parked`
  (cold, with revisit trigger). Run `ledger_lint.py` after edits.
- **Named-actor rule.** A lead with no specific actor, date, and record ID cannot pass triage.
  "Spend on issue X spiked" is a dataset summary; name who, when, and which filings.
- **DECISIONS.md** gets a row for every human intervention (triage picks, kills, editorial and
  legal-flag calls) with the trace file it happened in.
- **Traces.** Every working session is exported to `traces/YYYY-MM-DD_<skill-or-phase>_<lead>.jsonl`
  (session JSONL lives under `~/.claude/projects/<project-slug>/`) and indexed in `traces/INDEX.md`.
- **Findings lock only after verification.** A fresh session (no drafting context) re-derives
  every claim from cited records per `skills/finding-verifier/SKILL.md`, then the lock is logged
  in `DECISIONS.md`. The findings report is assembled from locked findings only.
- **Model/budget tiers.** Extraction and filtering stay in Python/SQL. Cheap models scan and rank
  (returning record IDs + one-line hypotheses, never quoted record text). Frontier models
  deep-read lead-attached records (cap ~30 records/session) and verify findings.
- **Exploratory outside-data checks (novelty/news-landscape) follow a fixed protocol,
  separate from finding-verifier's rigor** — anchor the search window to the actual event
  date (not "today"), run novelty and landscape checks as separate bounded passes (~3
  queries each), distill to a conclusion before it reaches the ledger. Full protocol in
  `skills/outside-context-scan/SKILL.md`. Established 2026-07-06 after searching
  "GlobalFoundries news 2026" for a 2024 event; deliberately kept out of
  `skills/finding-verifier/SKILL.md` since "verifier" implies a rigor this exploratory
  research doesn't have and shouldn't claim.
- **Self-contained language.** Submitted artifacts (SKILL.md files, findings, README) must stand
  alone — no references to internal planning docs or prior processes.

## Data facts that bite (verified against real records 2026-07-04)

- House quarterly XML uses `<alis><ali_info>` with `<issueAreaCode>` — NOT the `ali_Code` flat
  list the data manual describes (that may still apply to LD-1 registrations; unverified).
- Agencies lobbied are one comma-separated `<federal_agencies>` string per ali_info; splitting
  it is entity-resolver work.
- House forms pad with empty `<lobbyist>` slots — skip rows with no first and no last name.
- Senate registrant objects carry `house_registrant_id` — the clean Senate↔House crosswalk key.
- Senate LD-203 contributions are semiannual; early-year files are legitimately tiny.
- **Never sum filings without deduping — and the dedup key must be the period-invariant
  field, not the type code, applied identically on both chambers.** Registrants file
  duplicates (identical Senate Q1s posted 22 seconds apart) and amendments (Senate
  `filing_type` codes like `1A`/`2A`/`3A`/`4A`; House refilings under new filing_ids).
  Filtering senate to `filing_type LIKE 'Q%'` SILENTLY DROPS amendments — this bit twice:
  2026-07-04's un-deduped version fabricated a "House reports 2× Senate" pattern, and
  2026-07-06's H1c/H1d v1 (which filtered/partitioned by `filing_type`) fabricated a
  "chronic 10x cross-chamber mis-reporter" pattern that was actually senate's
  PRE-amendment figure vs house's POST-amendment figure (house wasn't affected because its
  directory-based period bucketing already folds originals+amendments together). Caught
  by a human cross-checking the live House portal, not by any internal check — a reminder
  that "the query ran and produced clean-looking numbers" is not verification. The correct
  pattern: dedup keyed on `filing_period` (constant across original+amendment, e.g.
  "second_quarter" for both `Q2` and `2A`), NOT `filing_type`, picking latest by `posted`/
  `filing_id` — see `queries/sweep_2026.sql#H1c` (current, fixed version) for the pattern.
- **Never sum senate + house datasets.** LD-2 quarterlies are filed with both chambers, so the
  two datasets are largely copies of the same filings. Dollar attribution is senate-primary
  (richer metadata); use house only to reconcile or fill gaps. Verified 2026-07-05 — the
  cross-dataset version (sweep#C1b) inflated per-bill totals ~40%.
- **House XML dumps are partial snapshots.** House 2026-Q1 holds 12,656 filings vs 21,145
  senate Q1s (the deadline-week flood is missing). Senate is the completeness reference for
  recent quarters; a filing absent house-side is expected noise, not a story.
- **Press and filings name bills differently.** Members write "the Farm Bill" / "NDAA"; filings
  cite H.R./S. numbers. Number-only matching fabricates "lobbied but publicly silent" bills
  (killed L004 that way). Say-vs-pay comparisons need alias matching or provision-level framing.
- **The Senate↔House join key is house `<senateID>` = `"<senate_registrant_id>-<senate_client_id>"`**
  (compound, engagement-level; verified 2026-07-06). Do NOT join senate `house_registrant_id`
  to house `<houseID>` — formats don't overlap (zero matches). Use `registrant_crosswalk`
  (entity-resolver) or the split_part pattern in `queries/sweep_2026.sql#H1c`.
- **`<houseID>` is NOT a resolvable filing_id/filename, and can collide with an unrelated
  one.** Verified 2026-07-06: a Mercury/PRVO Plinarsko Društvo quarterly filing carries
  `<houseID>301740622</houseID>`, but filing `301740622.xml` in this corpus is a completely
  different, unrelated registration (Cornerstone Government Affairs / National Association
  of Home Builders). `<houseID>` appears to be a persistent House-Clerk identifier for the
  registrant-client relationship (what the House disclosure portal's own search surfaces) —
  a different ID namespace from `filing_id` (the numeric filename), even though both are
  9-digit numbers that can coincidentally match an unrelated document. Never cite a
  `<houseID>` value as a `show_record.py` key; only the filename (`filing_id`) is a valid
  citation key on the House side.
- **Senate `client_id` is registrant-scoped, not global** — Comcast alone has 10+ client ids.
  Group clients by resolved entity (`entities`/`entity_aliases`, norm-key based), never by
  client_id. Registrant ids ARE global.
- Everything self-reported: strip whitespace everywhere, expect missing income/expenses, and
  treat gaps as potentially reportable rather than as noise.
- Windows: scripts force UTF-8 stdout (press text has curly quotes; pipes default to cp1252).

## Session discipline for Claude sessions in this repo

1. Start: read this file, `LEDGER.md`, and the tail of `DECISIONS.md`. Don't re-derive state.
2. Work one lead or one phase per session where practical (keeps traces reviewable).
3. End: update `LEDGER.md` (+ lint), log any human decisions, export the session trace, update
   `traces/INDEX.md`, commit.

Internal planning context (not part of the submission, not for citation in submitted artifacts):
`../_Plan.md` in the parent folder holds the phased plan and decision register.

**2026-07-06 reset:** `LEDGER.md`/`DECISIONS.md` were emptied and the prior pilot-scale content
moved to `archive/*_pilot-triage_2026-07-06.md`, so a session working the newly-built full corpus
(`db/lda_full.duckdb`) starts with a clean, unbiased view rather than inheriting pilot-era lead
framing. This was a deliberate one-time QA reset, not a standing practice — don't repeat it on a
future corpus rebuild without being asked. The archived files are historical reference only; do
not read them into context unless specifically asked to reconcile or restore something from them
(e.g. `findings/L010_pipe_materials_war.md` is untouched and still a real, independently-verified
finding regardless of this reset — it just isn't re-listed as a lead in the fresh ledger).

9	re-derived findings already on file from the pilot pass, because it ran the same canned lens file

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env python3
2	"""Build the GAIN investigation database from the raw challenge corpus.
3	
4	Parses three datasets (layout per the challenge data manual) into one DuckDB file:
5	
6	  <data-root>/congress_press/   JSONL press releases (year dirs + 2026 files at root)
7	  <data-root>/senate/           Senate LDA filings + contributions (JSON arrays, streamed)
8	  <data-root>/house/            House LDA registrations + quarterlies (one XML per filing)
9	
10	Guarantees:
11	  * Every row carries a raw-record pointer (src_file + src_line/src_index, or the XML
12	    path) so any query result resolves to a citable raw record in one step
13	    (see show_record.py).
14	  * A sanity report reconciles row counts against the data manual's published 2025
15	    scale before the database is trusted. Written next to the DB as sanity_report.md.
16	
17	Usage:
18	  python build_db.py --data-root data/                       # everything present
19	  python build_db.py --data-root data/ --years 2025 2026     # pilot slice
20	  python build_db.py --data-root data/ --sample 2025-Q1      # smoke mode (minutes)
21	
22	Caveat: Senate JSON key names and House XML tag names were drafted from the data
23	manual before the raw data landed and are coded defensively (multiple candidate
24	keys/tags, strip everything). The sanity report is the tripwire for mismatches —
25	verify against real data before trusting downstream queries.
26	"""
27	
28	import argparse
29	import json
30	import re
31	import sys
32	import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
33	from pathlib import Path
34	
35	import duckdb
36	import ijson
37	
38	BATCH = 50000
39	
40	# Sanity report contains non-ASCII; Windows pipes default to cp1252.
41	if hasattr(sys.stdout, "reconfigure"):
42	    sys.stdout.reconfigure(encoding="utf-8")
43	
44	# Data manual's published 2025 scale, used by the sanity report.
45	MANUAL_2025 = {
46	    "press_releases": 48000,        # "~48K releases in 2025"
47	    "senate_filings": 108225,
48	    "senate_contributions": 39438,
49	    "house_filings": 108522,        # ~7,762 registrations + ~100,760 quarterlies
50	}
51	
52	# Bill citations: longest alternatives first so "H.J.Res." doesn't match as "H.R.".
53	BILL_RE = re.compile(
54	    r"\b("
55	    r"H\.?\s*J\.?\s*RES\.?|S\.?\s*J\.?\s*RES\.?|"
56	    r"H\.?\s*CON\.?\s*RES\.?|S\.?\s*CON\.?\s*RES\.?|"
57	    r"H\.?\s*RES\.?|S\.?\s*RES\.?|"
58	    r"H\.?\s*R\.?|S\.?"
59	    r")\s*(\d{1,5})\b",
60	    re.IGNORECASE,
61	)
62	
63	
64	def norm_bill(prefix: str, number: str) -> str:
65	    return re.sub(r"[^A-Z]", "", prefix.upper()) + number
66	
67	
68	def extract_bills(text):
69	    if not text:
70	        return
71	    for m in BILL_RE.finditer(text):
72	        yield norm_bill(m.group(1), m.group(2)), m.group(0)
73	
74	
75	def get_any(d, *keys, default=None):
76	    """First non-empty value among candidate keys (Senate JSON field names vary)."""
77	    if not isinstance(d, dict):
78	        return default
79	    for k in keys:
80	        v = d.get(k)
81	        if v not in (None, "", [], {}):
82	            return v
83	    return default
84	
85	
86	def to_num(v):
87	    if v in (None, ""):
88	        return None
89	    try:
90	        return float(str(v).replace(",", ""))
91	    except ValueError:
92	        return None
93	
94	
95	def strip_or_none(v):
96	    if v is None:
97	        return None
98	    s = str(v).strip()
99	    return s or None
100	
101	
102	# ---------------------------------------------------------------- schema
103	
104	DDL = """
105	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS press_releases (
106	  pr_id BIGINT, url TEXT, title TEXT, date TEXT, date_source TEXT, source TEXT,
107	  domain TEXT, scraper TEXT, bioguide_id TEXT, member_name TEXT, party TEXT,
108	  state TEXT, chamber TEXT, text TEXT, src_file TEXT, src_line INTEGER);
109	
110	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS senate_filings (
111	  filing_uuid TEXT, filing_type TEXT, filing_period TEXT, filing_year INTEGER,
112	  income DOUBLE, expenses DOUBLE, registrant_id TEXT, registrant_name TEXT,
113	  house_registrant_id TEXT, client_id TEXT, client_name TEXT, client_state TEXT,
114	  client_description TEXT, posted TEXT, src_file TEXT, src_index INTEGER);
115	
116	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS senate_activities (
117	  filing_uuid TEXT, activity_index INTEGER, general_issue_code TEXT,
118	  description TEXT, src_file TEXT, src_index INTEGER);
119	
120	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS senate_lobbyists (
121	  filing_uuid TEXT, activity_index INTEGER, first_name TEXT, last_name TEXT,
122	  covered_position TEXT, is_new BOOLEAN, src_file TEXT, src_index INTEGER);
123	
124	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS senate_gov_entities (
125	  filing_uuid TEXT, activity_index INTEGER, entity_name TEXT,
126	  src_file TEXT, src_index INTEGER);
127	
128	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS senate_foreign_entities (
129	  filing_uuid TEXT, name TEXT, country TEXT, src_file TEXT, src_index INTEGER);
130	
131	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS senate_contributions (
132	  filing_uuid TEXT, filer_type TEXT, filing_year INTEGER, registrant_name TEXT,
133	  lobbyist_name TEXT, pacs TEXT, src_file TEXT, src_index INTEGER);
134	
135	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS senate_contribution_items (
136	  filing_uuid TEXT, item_index INTEGER, contribution_type TEXT, amount DOUBLE,
137	  payee TEXT, honoree TEXT, contributor_name TEXT, date TEXT,
138	  src_file TEXT, src_index INTEGER);
139	
140	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS house_filings (
141	  filing_id TEXT, form TEXT, organization_name TEXT, client_name TEXT,
142	  senate_reg_id TEXT, house_reg_id TEXT, report_year INTEGER, report_period TEXT,
143	  income DOUBLE, expenses DOUBLE, specific_issues TEXT, src_path TEXT);
144	
145	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS house_lobbyists (
146	  filing_id TEXT, ali_index INTEGER, first_name TEXT, last_name TEXT,
147	  covered_position TEXT, lobbyist_new TEXT, src_path TEXT);
148	
149	-- One row per <alis><ali_info> block (real quarterly schema, verified 2026-07-04).
150	-- federal_agencies is the raw comma-separated string. Splitting it into clean
151	-- entities is entity-resolver work, not loader work.
152	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS house_alis (
153	  filing_id TEXT, ali_index INTEGER, issue_code TEXT, specific_issues TEXT,
154	  federal_agencies TEXT, src_path TEXT);
155	
156	CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS bill_mentions (
157	  dataset TEXT, record_key TEXT, bill TEXT, raw_match TEXT, src TEXT);
158	"""
159	
160	VIEWS = """
161	CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW members AS
162	  SELECT bioguide_id, any_value(member_name) AS name, any_value(party) AS party,
163	         any_value(state) AS state, any_value(chamber) AS chamber,
164	         count(*) AS n_releases
165	  FROM press_releases WHERE bioguide_id IS NOT NULL GROUP BY bioguide_id;
166	
167	CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_spend_by_client_quarter AS
168	  SELECT client_name, filing_year, filing_period,
169	         sum(income) AS total_income, count(*) AS n_filings
170	  FROM senate_filings GROUP BY 1, 2, 3;
171	
172	-- Income is filing-level. Attributing it to each activity's issue code
173	-- overstates multi-issue filings. Use for ranking/trend, not for exact dollars.
174	CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_spend_by_issue_quarter AS
175	  SELECT a.general_issue_code, f.filing_year, f.filing_period,
176	         sum(f.income) AS attributed_income, count(DISTINCT f.filing_uuid) AS n_filings
177	  FROM senate_activities a JOIN senate_filings f USING (filing_uuid)
178	  GROUP BY 1, 2, 3;
179	
180	CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_releases_by_member_month AS
181	  SELECT bioguide_id, any_value(member_name) AS name, substr(date, 1, 7) AS month,
182	         count(*) AS n_releases
183	  FROM press_releases GROUP BY bioguide_id, substr(date, 1, 7);
184	
185	CREATE OR REPLACE VIEW v_covered_positions AS
186	  SELECT 'senate' AS dataset, filing_uuid AS record_key, first_name, last_name,
187	         covered_position FROM senate_lobbyists
188	  WHERE covered_position IS NOT NULL
189	  UNION ALL
190	  SELECT 'house', filing_id, first_name, last_name, covered_position
191	  FROM house_lobbyists WHERE covered_position IS NOT NULL;
192	"""
193	
194	
195	class Sink:
196	    """Batched inserts per table, staged through temp NDJSON files and COPY.
197	
198	    duckdb's executemany runs row-by-row (~100x slower). NDJSON staging is
199	    immune to the CSV-dialect ambiguity that multi-line press text triggers:
200	    json.dumps escapes every field onto one line.
201	    """
202	
203	    def __init__(self, con):
204	        self.con = con
205	        self.buf = {}
206	        self.counts = {}
207	        self.cols = {}
208	
209	    def _columns(self, table):
210	        if table not in self.cols:
211	            self.cols[table] = [r[1] for r in self.con.execute(
212	                f"PRAGMA table_info('{table}')").fetchall()]
213	        return self.cols[table]
214	
215	    def add(self, table, row):
216	        self.buf.setdefault(table, []).append(row)
217	        self.counts[table] = self.counts.get(table, 0) + 1
218	        if len(self.buf[table]) >= BATCH:
219	            self.flush(table)
220	
221	    def flush(self, table=None):
222	        import os
223	        import tempfile
224	        for t in [table] if table else list(self.buf):
225	            rows = self.buf.get(t) or []
226	            if not rows:
227	                continue
228	            cols = self._columns(t)
229	            fd, tmp = tempfile.mkstemp(suffix=".ndjson")
230	            os.close(fd)
231	            try:
232	                with open(tmp, "w", encoding="utf-8") as f:
233	                    for row in rows:
234	                        f.write(json.dumps(dict(zip(cols, row)),
235	                                           ensure_ascii=False, default=str))
236	                        f.write("\n")
237	                self.con.execute(
238	                    f"COPY {t} FROM '{tmp.replace(chr(92), '/')}' (FORMAT json)")
239	            finally:
240	                os.unlink(tmp)
241	            self.buf[t] = []
242	
243	
244	# ---------------------------------------------------------------- press
245	
246	def load_press(sink, data_root, years, months=None, max_records=None):
247	    root = data_root / "congress_press"
248	    files = sorted(root.glob("*.jsonl")) + sorted(root.glob("*/*.jsonl"))
249	    n = 0
250	    for path in files:
251	        m = re.match(r"(\d{4})-(\d{2})", path.stem)
252	        if not m or int(m.group(1)) not in years:
253	            continue
254	        if months and (int(m.group(1)), int(m.group(2))) not in months:
255	            continue
256	        rel = path.relative_to(data_root).as_posix()
257	        with open(path, encoding="utf-8") as f:
258	            for line_no, line in enumerate(f, 1):
259	                line = line.strip()
260	                if not line:
261	                    continue
262	                rec = json.loads(line)
263	                mem = rec.get("member") or {}
264	                n += 1
265	                key = f"{rel}:{line_no}"
266	                sink.add("press_releases", (
267	                    n, rec.get("url"), rec.get("title"), rec.get("date"),
268	                    rec.get("date_source"), rec.get("source"), rec.get("domain"),
269	                    rec.get("scraper"), get_any(mem, "bioguide_id"),
270	                    get_any(mem, "name"), get_any(mem, "party"),
271	                    get_any(mem, "state"), get_any(mem, "chamber"),
272	                    rec.get("text"), rel, line_no))
273	                for bill, raw in extract_bills(rec.get("text") or ""):
274	                    sink.add("bill_mentions", ("press", key, bill, raw, rel))
275	                if max_records and n >= max_records:
276	                    sink.flush()
277	                    return n
278	    sink.flush()
279	    return n
280	
281	
282	# ---------------------------------------------------------------- senate
283	
284	def load_senate(sink, data_root, years, max_records=None):
285	    done = {"filings": 0, "contributions": 0}
286	    for year in sorted(years):
287	        for kind in ("filings", "contributions"):
288	            if max_records and done[kind] >= max_records:
289	                continue
290	            path = data_root / "senate" / str(year) / kind / f"{kind}_{year}.json"
291	            if not path.exists():
292	                continue
293	            rel = path.relative_to(data_root).as_posix()
294	            with open(path, "rb") as f:
295	                for idx, rec in enumerate(ijson.items(f, "item")):
296	                    if kind == "filings":
297	                        _senate_filing(sink, rec, rel, idx)
298	                    else:
299	                        _senate_contribution(sink, rec, rel, idx)
300	                    done[kind] += 1
301	                    if max_records and done[kind] >= max_records:
302	                        break
303	    sink.flush()
304	    return done["filings"]
305	
306	
307	def _senate_filing(sink, rec, rel, idx):
308	    uuid = get_any(rec, "filing_uuid", "uuid")
309	    reg = rec.get("registrant") or {}
310	    cli = rec.get("client") or {}
311	    sink.add("senate_filings", (
312	        uuid, get_any(rec, "filing_type"), get_any(rec, "filing_period"),
313	        rec.get("filing_year"), to_num(get_any(rec, "income")),
314	        to_num(get_any(rec, "expenses")), strip_or_none(get_any(reg, "id")),
315	        strip_or_none(get_any(reg, "name")),
316	        strip_or_none(get_any(reg, "house_registrant_id")),
317	        strip_or_none(get_any(cli, "id", "client_id")),
318	        strip_or_none(get_any(cli, "name")), strip_or_none(get_any(cli, "state")),
319	        strip_or_none(get_any(cli, "general_description")),
320	        get_any(rec, "dt_posted", "posted"), rel, idx))
321	    for ai, act in enumerate(rec.get("lobbying_activities") or []):
322	        desc = strip_or_none(get_any(act, "description"))
323	        sink.add("senate_activities", (
324	            uuid, ai, get_any(act, "general_issue_code"), desc, rel, idx))
325	        for bill, raw in extract_bills(desc or ""):
326	            sink.add("bill_mentions", ("senate", uuid, bill, raw, rel))
327	        for lob in act.get("lobbyists") or []:
328	            person = lob.get("lobbyist") or lob
329	            sink.add("senate_lobbyists", (
330	                uuid, ai, strip_or_none(get_any(person, "first_name")),
331	                strip_or_none(get_any(person, "last_name")),
332	                strip_or_none(get_any(lob, "covered_position")),
333	                bool(get_any(lob, "new", "is_new", default=False)), rel, idx))
334	        for ge in act.get("government_entities") or []:
335	            name = ge.get("name") if isinstance(ge, dict) else ge
336	            sink.add("senate_gov_entities", (uuid, ai, strip_or_none(name), rel, idx))
337	    for fe in rec.get("foreign_entities") or []:
338	        if isinstance(fe, dict):
339	            sink.add("senate_foreign_entities", (
340	                uuid, strip_or_none(get_any(fe, "name")),
341	                strip_or_none(get_any(fe, "country")), rel, idx))
342	
343	
344	def _senate_contribution(sink, rec, rel, idx):
345	    uuid = get_any(rec, "filing_uuid", "uuid")
346	    reg = rec.get("registrant") or {}
347	    lob = rec.get("lobbyist") or {}
348	    lob_name = " ".join(x for x in (get_any(lob, "first_name"),
349	                                    get_any(lob, "last_name")) if x) or None
350	    pacs = rec.get("pacs") or []
351	    pacs_txt = "; ".join(p if isinstance(p, str) else str(get_any(p, "name", default=p))
352	                         for p in pacs) or None
353	    sink.add("senate_contributions", (
354	        uuid, get_any(rec, "filer_type"), rec.get("filing_year"),
355	        strip_or_none(get_any(reg, "name")), lob_name, pacs_txt, rel, idx))
356	    for ii, item in enumerate(rec.get("contribution_items") or []):
357	        sink.add("senate_contribution_items", (
358	            uuid, ii, get_any(item, "contribution_type", "type"),
359	            to_num(get_any(item, "amount")),
360	            strip_or_none(get_any(item, "payee_name", "payee")),
361	            strip_or_none(get_any(item, "honoree_name", "honoree")),
362	            strip_or_none(get_any(item, "contributor_name", "contributor")),
363	            get_any(item, "date", "contribution_date"), rel, idx))
364	
365	
366	# ---------------------------------------------------------------- house
367	
368	DIR_RE = re.compile(r"^(\d{4})_(Registrations|1stQuarter|2ndQuarter|3rdQuarter|4thQuarter)_XML$")
369	PERIOD = {"Registrations": "RR", "1stQuarter": "Q1", "2ndQuarter": "Q2",
370	          "3rdQuarter": "Q3", "4thQuarter": "Q4"}
371	
372	
373	def _strip_ns(root):
374	    for el in root.iter():
375	        if "}" in el.tag:
376	            el.tag = el.tag.split("}", 1)[1]
377	
378	
379	def _ft(root, *names):
380	    """First non-blank text among candidate tag names, searched anywhere."""
381	    for name in names:
382	        for el in root.iter(name):
383	            if el.text and el.text.strip():
384	                return el.text.strip()
385	    return None
386	
387	
388	def load_house(sink, data_root, years, periods=None, max_records=None):
389	    house = data_root / "house"
390	    n, errors = 0, []
391	    if not house.exists():
392	        return n, errors
393	    for d in sorted(house.iterdir()):
394	        m = DIR_RE.match(d.name) if d.is_dir() else None
395	        if not m or int(m.group(1)) not in years:
396	            continue
397	        year, period = int(m.group(1)), PERIOD[m.group(2)]
398	        if periods and period not in periods:
399	            continue
400	        for xml_path in sorted(d.glob("*.xml")):
401	            try:
402	                tree = ET.parse(xml_path)
403	            except ET.ParseError as e:
404	                errors.append(f"{xml_path.name}: {e}")
405	                continue
406	            root = tree.getroot()
407	            _strip_ns(root)
408	            fid = xml_path.stem
409	            rel = xml_path.relative_to(data_root).as_posix()
410	            form = "LD1" if root.tag.endswith("1") else "LD2"
411	
412	            def add_lobbyists(parent, ali_index):
413	                for lob in parent.iter("lobbyist"):
414	                    first = _ft(lob, "lobbyistFirstName", "firstName", "first_name")
415	                    last = _ft(lob, "lobbyistLastName", "lastName", "last_name")
416	                    if not (first or last):
417	                        continue  # forms pad with empty <lobbyist> slots
418	                    sink.add("house_lobbyists", (
419	                        fid, ali_index, first, last,
420	                        _ft(lob, "coveredPosition", "covered_position"),
421	                        _ft(lob, "lobbyistNew", "new"), rel))
422	
423	            all_desc = []
424	            ali_infos = root.findall(".//alis/ali_info")
425	            if ali_infos:
426	                # Quarterly (LD2) schema, verified against real 2026-Q1 files.
427	                for ai, ali in enumerate(ali_infos):
428	                    descs = [d.text.strip() for d in ali.iter("description")
429	                             if d.text and d.text.strip()]
430	                    desc = "\n".join(descs) or None
431	                    if desc:
432	                        all_desc.append(desc)
433	                    sink.add("house_alis", (
434	                        fid, ai, _ft(ali, "issueAreaCode", "ali_Code"), desc,
435	                        _ft(ali, "federal_agencies", "federalAgencies"), rel))
436	                    add_lobbyists(ali, ai)
437	            else:
438	                # Registration (LD1) / older layout per the data manual: flat
439	                # ali_Code list, lobbyists at document level. Verify when a
440	                # Registrations_XML directory lands.
441	                for ai, ali in enumerate(root.iter("ali_Code")):
442	                    if ali.text and ali.text.strip():
443	                        sink.add("house_alis", (fid, ai, ali.text.strip(),
444	                                                None, None, rel))
445	                descs = [d.text.strip() for d in root.findall(".//specific_issues//description")
446	                         if d.text and d.text.strip()]
447	                descs += [el.text.strip() for el in root.findall(".//specific_issues")
448	                          if el.text and el.text.strip()]
449	                all_desc.extend(descs)
450	                add_lobbyists(root, None)
451	
452	            spec = "\n".join(all_desc) or None
453	            sink.add("house_filings", (
454	                fid, form, _ft(root, "organizationName", "organizationname"),
455	                _ft(root, "clientName", "clientname"),
456	                _ft(root, "senateID", "senateId", "senateid"),
457	                _ft(root, "houseID", "houseId", "houseid"),
458	                year, period, to_num(_ft(root, "income")),
459	                to_num(_ft(root, "expenses")), spec, rel))
460	            for bill, raw in extract_bills(spec or ""):
461	                sink.add("bill_mentions", ("house", fid, bill, raw, rel))
462	            n += 1
463	            if max_records and n >= max_records:
464	                sink.flush()
465	                return n, errors
466	    sink.flush()
467	    return n, errors
468	
469	
470	# ---------------------------------------------------------------- sanity
471	
472	def sanity_report(con, db_path, years, house_errors):
473	    lines = ["# Sanity report", ""]
474	    tables = [r[0] for r in con.execute(
475	        "SELECT table_name FROM information_schema.tables "
476	        "WHERE table_type='BASE TABLE' ORDER BY 1").fetchall()]
477	    lines.append("| table | rows |")
478	    lines.append("|---|---|")
479	    counts = {}
480	    for t in tables:
481	        c = con.execute(f"SELECT count(*) FROM {t}").fetchone()[0]
482	        counts[t] = c
483	        lines.append(f"| {t} | {c:,} |")
484	    lines.append("")
485	    if 2025 in years:
486	        lines.append("## 2025 vs data manual")
487	        lines.append("")
488	        lines.append("| dataset | loaded (2025) | manual says | delta |")
489	        lines.append("|---|---|---|---|")
490	        loaded = {
491	            "press_releases": con.execute(
492	                "SELECT count(*) FROM press_releases WHERE src_file LIKE '%2025-%'"
493	            ).fetchone()[0],
494	            "senate_filings": con.execute(
495	                "SELECT count(*) FROM senate_filings WHERE filing_year=2025"
496	            ).fetchone()[0],
497	            "senate_contributions": con.execute(
498	                "SELECT count(*) FROM senate_contributions WHERE filing_year=2025"
499	            ).fetchone()[0],
500	            "house_filings": con.execute(
501	                "SELECT count(*) FROM house_filings WHERE report_year=2025"
502	            ).fetchone()[0],
503	        }
504	        for k, ref in MANUAL_2025.items():
505	            got = loaded[k]
506	            delta = (got - ref) / ref * 100 if ref else 0
507	            flag = "" if abs(delta) < 10 else "  ⚠️"
508	            lines.append(f"| {k} | {got:,} | ~{ref:,} | {delta:+.1f}%{flag} |")
509	        lines.append("")
510	        lines.append("Deltas beyond ±10% mean either a partial download or a parser "
511	                     "mismatch — investigate before trusting downstream queries.")
512	    if house_errors:
513	        lines.append("")
514	        lines.append(f"## House XML parse errors: {len(house_errors)}")
515	        lines.extend(f"- {e}" for e in house_errors[:20])
516	    report = "\n".join(lines) + "\n"
517	    out = db_path.parent / "sanity_report.md"
518	    out.write_text(report, encoding="utf-8")
519	    print(report)
520	    print(f"Sanity report written to {out}")
521	
522	
523	# ---------------------------------------------------------------- main
524	
525	def main():
526	    ap = argparse.ArgumentParser(description=__doc__,
527	                                 formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter)
528	    ap.add_argument("--data-root", required=True, type=Path)
529	    ap.add_argument("--db", type=Path, default=Path("db/lda.duckdb"))
530	    ap.add_argument("--years", nargs="*", type=int,
531	                    help="Years to load (default: everything present)")
532	    ap.add_argument("--sample", metavar="YYYY-QN",
533	                    help="Smoke mode: one quarter of press+house, capped Senate records")
534	    ap.add_argument("--max-records", type=int,
535	                    help="Cap per-dataset primary records (smoke/testing)")
536	    args = ap.parse_args()
537	
538	    data_root = args.data_root
539	    if not data_root.exists():
540	        sys.exit(f"data root not found: {data_root}")
541	
542	    years = set(args.years or range(2022, 2027))
543	    months = periods = None
544	    max_records = args.max_records
545	    if args.sample:
546	        m = re.match(r"^(\d{4})-Q([1-4])$", args.sample)
547	        if not m:
548	            sys.exit("--sample must look like 2025-Q1")
549	        y, q = int(m.group(1)), int(m.group(2))
550	        years = {y}
551	        months = {(y, mm) for mm in range(3 * q - 2, 3 * q + 1)}
552	        periods = {"RR", f"Q{q}"}
553	        max_records = max_records or 5000
554	
555	    args.db.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
556	    con = duckdb.connect(str(args.db))
557	    for stmt in DDL.split(";"):
558	        if stmt.strip():
559	            con.execute(stmt)
560	
561	    import time as _time
562	    sink = Sink(con)
563	    t0 = _time.time()
564	    print(f"Loading press releases ({sorted(years)}) ...")
565	    n_press = load_press(sink, data_root, years, months, max_records)
566	    t1 = _time.time()
567	    print(f"  {n_press:,} releases in {t1 - t0:.1f}s")
568	    print("Loading Senate LDA ...")
569	    n_sen = load_senate(sink, data_root, years, max_records)
570	    t2 = _time.time()
571	    print(f"  {n_sen:,} filings in {t2 - t1:.1f}s")
572	    print("Loading House LDA ...")
573	    n_house, house_errors = load_house(sink, data_root, years, periods, max_records)
574	    t3 = _time.time()
575	    print(f"  {n_house:,} filings ({len(house_errors)} parse errors) in {t3 - t2:.1f}s")
576	
577	    for stmt in VIEWS.split(";"):
578	        if stmt.strip():
579	            con.execute(stmt)
580	
581	    sanity_report(con, args.db, years, house_errors)
582	    con.close()
583	    print(f"Done: {args.db}")
584	
585	
586	if __name__ == "__main__":
587	    main()
